mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns more than 50% of the time, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during low-inflation periods yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic momentum that translates into immediate positive market sentiment and higher S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC consistently below 0.01) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between real GDP growth and the federal funds rate indicates accommodative policy relative to economic output, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-FEDFUNDS spread interaction term produces negative 60-day forward returns in over 55% of observations, or loses predictive significance when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real personal consumption expenditures exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp consumer pullback that forces corporate earnings revisions and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 50% of instances where RSAFS drops below the 0.5% threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of rising unemployment confirms a synchronized economic contraction that triggers immediate defensive equity selling within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during high unemployment periods yield positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the mechanism fails to predict direction during actual NBER-dated recessions.
